name: Association of Southeast Asian Nations

governance.decision_making: ASEAN Way: consensus and non-interference in member states' internal affairs

strategic_priorities: Navigating US-China rivalry without choosing sides — ASEAN centrality
South China Sea: managing overlapping claims with China (Philippines, Vietnam, Malaysia, Brunei)
ASEAN Economic Community deepening: services liberalization and digital trade
Supply chain diversification: attracting manufacturing from China (China+1 strategy)
Myanmar crisis: ASEAN Five-Point Consensus largely stalled; humanitarian corridor negotiations
Regional AI and digital economy governance framework
summary: ASEAN is the world's fifth-largest economy and a critical node in global supply chains, positioned at the center of the US-China strategic rivalry. The bloc's consensus-based decision-making and non-interference norm limit collective action, but individual members have become major beneficiaries of supply chain diversification from China. The South China Sea remains the foremost security flashpoint, with the Philippines increasingly willing to contest Chinese encroachment with US backing.
recent_developments: Philippines-China tensions in the South China Sea escalated in 2025, with multiple water cannon incidents at Second Thomas Shoal; ASEAN issued unusually direct calls for restraint
Vietnam, Malaysia, and Indonesia attracted combined FDI inflows of over $90 billion in 2025, driven by semiconductor, EV battery, and electronics supply chain relocation
ASEAN-GCC summit held in 2025 deepened Gulf-Southeast Asia energy and investment ties; Saudi Aramco expanded downstream presence in Malaysia and Indonesia
Malaysia's 2025 chairmanship advanced the ASEAN Digital Economy Framework Agreement (DEFA), targeting $2 trillion digital economy by 2030
Myanmar junta's control fragmented further in 2025 as resistance forces held significant territory; ASEAN suspended Myanmar's participation in most political meetings
risks: South China Sea disputes risk escalation, particularly involving the Philippines under US-Philippines Mutual Defense Treaty
Myanmar instability generating refugee flows and undermining ASEAN's international credibility
Overreliance on Chinese demand — China accounts for 18% of ASEAN exports
Internal divergence on China policy: Cambodia and Laos closely aligned with Beijing, limiting bloc consensus
